title: Assess BlockState type change impact across all subsystems
description:
## Source
Council review at `Data/Pages/council/blueprint-yaml-autonomy-council-2026-06-29.md` — Blocking issue B4  
Blueprint YAML design doc — Phase 0 deliverables

## Background
The blueprint YAML design proposes changing `PlacementBlock.BlockState` from `string?` (flat string like `"half=top"`) to a structured `BlockState` class with `Dictionary<string, string> Properties`. This is a breaking change that affects:

1. `Agent.Construction/BlueprintSchema.cs` — `PlacementBlock` record definition
2. `Agent.Construction/BlueprintParser.cs` — legend parser annotates BlockState as flat string
3. `Agent.Construction/BlueprintExecutor.cs` — passes `blockState` as `arguments["blockState"]` string
4. `MineflayerAdapter/index.js` — receives `args.blockState` as string, applies to block
5. `Agent.Planning/Goals/BuildGoal.cs` — stores `IReadOnlyList<PlacementBlock>`
6. All test files that construct `PlacementBlock` instances

## Scope
Complete a full inventory of every code location that reads, writes, or expects `PlacementBlock.BlockState`. For each location, determine:
- Is it a source (writes the field)?
- Is it a consumer (reads the field)?
- Would changing to `BlockState` object require a serialization format (string ↔ dict converter)?
- What is the migration effort per location?
- Can we keep the flat string as a serialization format and add structured access as an overlay?

## Deliverables
- Complete inventory document (all file paths + line numbers)
- Migration effort estimate per subsystem
- Recommendation: structured `BlockState` class vs. flat string with helper methods
- Impact on adapter wire protocol (Node.js)

## Exit Criteria
- All 6+ subsystems inventoried
- Migration effort estimated (days per subsystem)
- Decision documented: structured class or flat string with helpers
- No accidental `BlockState` changes until this assessment is complete
